qa_pairs.question: For a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application, if a research project requires 180 core-years of CPU and 50 TB of project storage on the Fir cluster, which of these resources should be formally requested through the RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: If a project requires 180 core-years of CPU and 50 TB of project storage on the Fir cluster, the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application should only request the 50 TB of project storage. The CPU request should not be included, as it may be possible to obtain those CPU resources without a RAC allocation.

qa_pairs.question: Why might a researcher be advised not to request certain computational resources, such as CPU core-years, in their Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application, even if their project needs them?
qa_pairs.answer: A researcher might be advised not to request certain computational resources, such as CPU core-years, in their Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application if the amount needed is below the minimum eligibility threshold for a RAC allocation. In such cases, these resources may be obtainable through other service mechanisms outside of the RAC process.
